info.name: text-insert
info.text: His theory lacks a solid foundation.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Theories can't stand without a solid foundation.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: His theory is a pile of rubble.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Failed theories litter the past like so many piles of rubble.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: His theory has become a fortress.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: The strongest of theories cease to be a house, and become a fortress.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: We need to tear down the faulty scaffolding before we can continue construction.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: For theories to move forward, we must tear down any faulty scaffolding before continuing construction.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: That theory of yours is no match for my edifice of facts.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Even the most well constructed theory is no match for an edifice of facts.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: You have to start with the foundations before you build any further.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: You have to start with the foundations before you build any further.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: Since it is no longer possible to dismantle the building, we may as well develop it.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Since it is no longer possible to dismantle the building, we may as well develop it.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: We will systematically tear your theories down.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: We will systematically tear your theories down.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Sometimes theories must be torn down so that new ones can be constructed.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: Construction of that theory will take years.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Construction of a truly useful theory can take the lifetimes of many generations of builders.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: I need to build the new theory from the ground up.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: I need to build the new theory from the ground up.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: A complicated theory is as difficult to construct, and easy to demolish, as a high-rise.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: We're going to need a bigger house.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Sometimes  to need a bigger house.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: The construction is subtle and sophisticated.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: The construction is subtle and sophisticated.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Sometimes the best theories have the simplest construction.
